items.title: Streamline Native Login Flow in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses an issue on iOS Capacitor where `window.location.reload()` could leave users on a blank screen after login. Instead, the auth panel is now hidden and users are navigated to the home page using the Vue router, enhancing the login experience on native platforms. This change works in conjunction with a companion PR to fully resolve the iOS login flow.

items.title: Fix silent login failure on iOS Capacitor in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a silent login failure in the Nexior app on iOS Capacitor due to a `postMessage` targetOrigin mismatch. The fix modifies the `crossWindow.ts` file to default the targetOrigin to `'*'` when no usable HTTP referrer is available, ensuring that the message is correctly sent to the parent window. Security is maintained as the receiver validates the event origin before processing.

items.title: Enhance Nginx Caching for SPA Shells in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the Nginx configuration to add a specific location block for `/index.html`, ensuring that responses include `Cache-Control: private`. This change prevents the Edge Optimizer from caching the SPA shell for the root path, allowing Googlebot to receive the prerendered page instead. Additionally, a note is included to clear the EO cache for affected paths after deployment.

items.title: Mobile App Name Change to AceData in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: The mobile app display name has been successfully changed from 'Nexior' to 'AceData' across all platforms. Key files modified include the app configuration, HTML title, and iOS/Android resource files to reflect this new branding. This update enhances brand consistency and user recognition.

items.title: Update Podfile.lock for Capacitor 7.5.0 in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update aligns the `Podfile.lock` with the current version of Capacitor 7.5.0, replacing the previous version 6.1.1. This change resolves the Xcode sandbox sync error encountered during builds, enhancing the development experience.

items.title: Enhance Cache-Control for SPA Shell in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the Nginx configuration to include 'private' in the Cache-Control header for the SPA shell. This change prevents shared caches from storing the shell, ensuring that Googlebot receives the prerendered page instead of the cached shell. The new header configuration will enhance SEO by delivering the correct content to search engine crawlers.

items.title: Upgrade Gradle, CocoaPods, and Capacitor in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the Nexior project by upgrading key dependencies. The Android Gradle Plugin has been updated to version 8.13.2, and the Gradle wrapper is now at 8.13. Additionally, Capacitor has been upgraded to 7.5.0 along with CocoaPods to version 1.16.2. A Javadoc comment has also been added to the MainActivity for better documentation.

items.title: Fix Android Login Flow and Site Configuration in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves issues with the Android login flow by ensuring users are directed to the official site instead of a site configuration page. Key changes include modifying the site origin retrieval to use the official hub URL for native platforms and skipping the site config page redirect after login. These improvements enhance the user experience for Android and iOS users.

items.title: Enhance CDN Handling for Prerendered Pages in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses an issue where the EdgeOne CDN was caching prerendered HTML for regular browser requests, leading to incorrect content delivery. By adding a `Cache-Control: private, no-store` header to the nginx configuration, we ensure that only Googlebot receives the prerendered content while regular browsers get the lightweight SPA shell. This change improves the user experience and maintains the integrity of the content served.

items.title: Fix stream consumption issue in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a critical issue in the `write_response_status()` function that caused streaming requests to fail with 500 errors due to premature stream consumption. The fix ensures that `aread()` is only called for non-200 error responses, allowing successful streaming responses to be processed correctly. Unit tests have been added to cover various scenarios, ensuring robust functionality moving forward.

items.title: Fix login flow on Android to prevent Chrome redirection in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a critical issue where the login process on Android opened Chrome instead of handling it within the app, leading to a dead localhost page. Key changes include modifying the logout action to show an in-app login popup and adjusting the base URL to prevent broken redirects. These fixes enhance the user experience by ensuring a seamless authentication flow on native platforms.

items.title: Fix SPA 404 on Refresh in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This hotfix resolves hard 404 errors on all SPA routes when users refresh the page. The issue stemmed from a bug in nginx 1.28.2 related to multiple 'if' blocks, which prevented the proper handling of requests. The solution involved replacing conditional logic with map directives and ensuring that the SPA fallback is always served correctly.

items.title: Fix CI Docker Build by Handling Unset PRERENDER_TOKEN in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a CI Docker build issue caused by the docker-compose configuration requiring the PRERENDER_TOKEN to be set. The change modifies the environment variable handling, allowing the build to proceed even when the token is unset. This enhancement improves the CI process and ensures smoother deployments.

items.title: Boosting SEO with Enhanced Metadata in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces significant SEO enhancements for the PlatformFrontend, including the addition of Open Graph tags, JSON-LD structured data, and canonical URLs. A unified Nginx configuration and a switch to hosted Prerender.io improve rendering efficiency and fallback behavior. The changes ensure better visibility and performance for search engines, enhancing user experience across the platform.

items.title: Boost SEO with Enhanced Sitemap and Robots.txt in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the sitemap.xml and robots.txt files to improve SEO crawling. Key changes include the addition of a homepage entry with a priority of 1.0 and weekly change frequency for all entries in the sitemap. The robots.txt file now disallows certain paths while allowing access to documents, services, and earnings, optimizing crawler efficiency.

items.title: Translate Chinese Error Messages to English in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances error handling by translating Chinese error messages to English before they are thrown. Key changes were made in multiple handler files to ensure that API responses consistently return English error messages, improving clarity for users. This will streamline error classification and enhance the overall user experience.

items.title: Enhance Midjourney Error Handling in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update improves error classification for the Midjourney API by accurately categorizing specific Chinese error messages. The changes ensure that 'Prompt参数错误' is treated as a BadRequestError (400) and '图片未通过审核' as a ForbiddenError (403) across all four Midjourney handlers. This adjustment aims to reduce the occurrence of misleading 500 errors, enhancing user experience and system reliability.

items.title: Boost aiohttp client size to 10MB for smoother uploads in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update increases the aiohttp client_max_size from 1MB to 10MB for the /record endpoint. This change addresses 413 errors encountered when posting large payloads, such as Claude Code requests with approximately 580KB system prompts. The adjustment enhances the platform's ability to handle larger data submissions effectively.
